question_text: Two identical kettles boil water at different locations. Kettle A is in a room at 20°C and Kettle B is in a room at 25°C. Both have the same efficiency rating of 80%. Which kettle wastes more energy as heat to the surroundings?

options.label: A
options.text: Kettle A, because the cooler room temperature increases relative heat loss

options.label: B
options.text: Kettle B, because the warmer room absorbs more energy

options.label: C
options.text: Both waste the same amount, because they have identical efficiency ratings and inputs

options.label: D
options.text: Neither wastes heat if their efficiency is 80%

correct_answer: Both waste the same amount, because they have identical efficiency ratings and inputs
explanation: Efficiency depends on the device and its input, not the surrounding temperature. If both kettles receive the same electrical energy input and have 80% efficiency, they each waste the same 20% as heat. Room temperature affects how quickly heat dissipates, but not how much the device wastes.
